error_weight_drug_volume: Please enter weight, drug total and volume
settings_instruction: Select calculators to display in sidebar:
checklist_note: For reference only. Does not replace clinical training and supervision.
settings_select: Select tools to show in sidebar:
ag_warn_albumin: Albumin-corrected AG provided for reference. Clinical correlation required.
ag_ref: Reference range approx 8-16 mmol/L; Delta Gap uses normal AG of 12 mmol/L.
dg_warn_zero: HCO3 is exactly 24 mmol/L. Delta Gap denominator is 0. Returning 0.
dg_interp_gt2: Delta Ratio > 2: High AG metabolic acidosis + concurrent metabolic alkalosis.
dg_interp_1_2: Delta Ratio 1-2: Pure high AG metabolic acidosis.
dg_interp_04_1: Delta Ratio 0.4-1: Mixed high AG and hyperchloremic metabolic acidosis.
dg_interp_lt04: Delta Ratio < 0.4: Predominantly hyperchloremic (non-AG) metabolic acidosis.
dg_ref: Structured assessment for mixed acid-base disorders. Interpret with clinical context.
winter_interp_base: Correlate with actual PCO2 to assess compensation.
winter_warn_boundary: Compensation judgment is close to the boundary (<= %.1f mmHg). Interpret with caution.
winter_interp_low: Actual PCO2 is lower than expected. Possible concurrent respiratory alkalosis.
winter_interp_high: Actual PCO2 is higher than expected. Possible concurrent respiratory acidosis.
winter_ref: Respiratory compensation check for metabolic acidosis.
na_warn_rate: Correction rate exceeds %.2f mmol/L/h. Risk of rapid correction.
osm_warn_high: Estimated osmolarity %.1f mOsm/L exceeds threshold (>320). Compare with measured osmolarity and gap.
osm_ref: Estimate only. Correlate with measured osmolarity.
fluid_warn_high: Calculated rate %.0f mL/h exceeds usual maintenance limits (<=100-120). Not for routine adult use.
fluid_warn_weight: Weight %.1f kg exceeds typical pediatric range (>40 kg). Use with caution.
err_amount_volume_num: Amount and volume must be valid numbers.
err_volume_gt_zero: Volume must be greater than zero.
err_weight_dose_num: Weight and dose must be valid numbers.
err_weight_dose_gt_zero: Weight and dose must be greater than zero.
antibiotic_desc: Weight %s kg @ %s mg/kg = %.2f mg per dose
err_weight_num: Weight must be a valid number.
err_weight_gt_zero: Weight must be greater than zero.
insulin_desc: Weight %s kg → Estimated TDD %.1f U (Basal %.1f U, Bolus %.1f U)
err_weight_tbsa_num: Weight and TBSA must be valid numbers.
parkland_desc:
24h Total: %.0f mL
First 8h Rate: %.0f mL/h
Next 16h Rate: %.0f mL/h
qsofa_guidance: quick SOFA identifies patients with suspected infection at greater risk for a poor outcome outside the ICU. 2 or more points suggests a greater risk.
gcs_guidance: GCS is used to objectively describe the extent of impaired consciousness in all types of acute medical and trauma patients.
app.title: MedPulse
chk_lumbar_puncture_item_0: Confirm indications and contraindications (e.g. no signs of elevated ICP)
chk_lumbar_puncture_item_3: Correct patient positioning (left lateral decubitus, knees to chest) and identify interspace (L3-L4 or L4-L5)
chk_lumbar_puncture_item_4: Operator hand hygiene, sterile gloves, establish sterile field with drapes
chk_lumbar_puncture_item_5: Local anesthesia with 2% Lidocaine layer by layer
chk_lumbar_puncture_item_6: Measure opening pressure, collect CSF (3-4 tubes, 1-2 mL each for routine, biochem, microbiology), measure closing pressure
chk_lumbar_puncture_item_7: Remove needle, apply pressure dressing, instruct patient to lie flat for 4-6 hours
chk_thoracentesis_item_0: Confirm indications, rule out contraindications, review CXR or US for fluid/air location
chk_thoracentesis_item_3: Identify puncture site (usually 7-9th intercostal space at scapular line, insert needle over superior rib margin)
chk_thoracentesis_item_4: Standard skin prep, sterile gloves, sterile drape
chk_thoracentesis_item_5: Local anesthesia with 2% Lidocaine down to parietal pleura
chk_thoracentesis_item_6: Aspirate fluid/air, max 600 mL initially, then max 1000 mL per session to prevent re-expansion pulmonary edema
chk_thoracentesis_item_7: Monitor vital signs continuously during procedure
chk_paracentesis_item_0: Empty bladder, confirm indications and rule out contraindications (e.g. severe bowel distension, pregnancy)
chk_paracentesis_item_3: Identify site: LLQ 1/3 distance from umbilicus to ASIS, or lateral decubitus at umbilicus level
chk_paracentesis_item_5: Local anesthesia with 2% Lidocaine down to parietal peritoneum
chk_paracentesis_item_6: Insert needle, collect sample or drain fluid slowly (prevent hepatic encephalopathy and hypotension)
chk_paracentesis_item_7: Remove needle, apply pressure and sterile dressing, consider abdominal binder if needed
chk_central_venous_catheter_item_0: Confirm indications and site (subclavian, internal jugular, or femoral)
chk_central_venous_catheter_item_3: Use bedside ultrasound guidance if available
chk_central_venous_catheter_item_4: Maximal sterile barrier precautions (hand hygiene, gown, gloves, mask, full body drape)
chk_central_venous_catheter_item_6: Aspirate dark venous blood, insert guidewire without resistance
chk_central_venous_catheter_item_7: Advance catheter over guidewire, remove guidewire, confirm blood return in all lumens, flush with heparin saline
chk_central_venous_catheter_item_8: Suture catheter in place, apply sterile transparent dressing
chk_central_venous_catheter_item_9: Post-procedure CXR (for IJ/SCV) to confirm tip position and rule out pneumothorax
chk_endotracheal_intubation_item_0: Assess airway (Mallampati, thyromental distance), prepare difficult airway cart (LMA, cricothyrotomy kit)
chk_endotracheal_intubation_item_1: ECG, SpO2, and NIBP connected and monitoring
chk_endotracheal_intubation_item_3: BVM connected to high-flow oxygen, preoxygenate with 100% O2 for at least 3 minutes
chk_endotracheal_intubation_item_4: Prepare and check laryngoscope (light working), ET tube (cuff intact), and stylet
chk_endotracheal_intubation_item_5: Prepare induction agents and muscle relaxants, ensure reliable IV access
chk_endotracheal_intubation_item_7: Confirm placement: bilateral breath sounds, no epigastric sounds, ETCO2 waveform present
chk_endotracheal_intubation_item_8: Secure ET tube, note depth at teeth
chk_endotracheal_intubation_item_9: Obtain post-intubation CXR to confirm tip position (3-5 cm above carina)
chk_chest_tube_insertion_item_0: Confirm pneumothorax or effusion extent and indication for tube thoracostomy
chk_chest_tube_insertion_item_3: Identify site: 2nd ICS mid-clavicular for air; 6-8th ICS mid/posterior axillary for fluid
chk_chest_tube_insertion_item_5: Local anesthesia with 2% Lidocaine down to parietal pleura
chk_chest_tube_insertion_item_6: Incision over superior rib margin, blunt dissection through muscle and pleura
chk_chest_tube_insertion_item_7: Insert chest tube, ensure all side holes are within pleural cavity
chk_chest_tube_insertion_item_8: Connect to water seal system, observe for air leak or fluid drainage and tidaling
chk_chest_tube_insertion_item_9: Suture tube in place, apply sterile dressing
chk_chest_tube_insertion_item_10: Post-procedure CXR to confirm tube position and lung re-expansion
abg_boundary_warning: Near formula boundary (%s = %.2f, boundary = %.2f %s). Rounding or sampling error may alter classification; correlate with clinical history.
abg_resp_acidosis_acute: HCO3 consistent with acute respiratory acidosis compensation.
abg_resp_acidosis_chronic: HCO3 consistent with chronic respiratory acidosis compensation.
abg_resp_acidosis_mismatch: HCO3 does not fully match simple respiratory acidosis compensation. Consider mixed disorder.
abg_resp_alkalosis_acute: HCO3 consistent with acute respiratory alkalosis compensation.
abg_resp_alkalosis_chronic: HCO3 consistent with chronic respiratory alkalosis compensation.
abg_resp_alkalosis_mismatch: HCO3 does not fully match simple respiratory alkalosis compensation. Consider mixed disorder.
abg_met_alkalosis_match: Respiratory compensation consistent with metabolic alkalosis.
abg_met_alkalosis_mismatch: PCO2 does not fully match simple metabolic alkalosis compensation. Consider mixed disorder.
abg_step4: Step 4. Mixed disorders: If compensation does not match expected, review AG, Delta Gap, and clinical context.
abg_comp_more_info: More information needed to assess compensation.
abg_comp_mixed_acidosis: Both PCO2 and HCO3 support acidosis. Consider mixed disorder.
abg_comp_mixed_alkalosis: Both PCO2 and HCO3 support alkalosis. Consider mixed disorder.
abg_primary_normal_mixed1: pH near normal, but metabolic acidosis / respiratory alkalosis pattern present
abg_primary_normal_mixed2: pH near normal, but metabolic alkalosis / respiratory acidosis pattern present
abg_comp_normal_mixed: May be compensated to near-normal, or may be a mixed disorder.
abg_comp_none: PCO2 and HCO3 do not show significant deviation.
err_unsupported_mass_unit: Unsupported mass unit. Use mcg/μg/ug or mg.
err_unsupported_conc_unit: Unsupported concentration unit. Use mcg/mL or mg/mL.
err_missing_value: Please enter a valid number for {field}
abg_class_formula: pH < 7.35 -> acidosis; pH > 7.45 -> alkalosis; HCO₃ < 22 -> metabolic acidosis; HCO₃ > 26 -> metabolic alkalosis; PCO₂ > 45 -> respiratory acidosis; PCO₂ < 35 -> respiratory alkalosis
abg_class_subst: pH %.2f -> %s; PCO₂ %.1f mmHg and HCO₃ %.1f mmol/L -> %s
abg_resp_acid_formula: Respiratory acidosis compensation: acute HCO₃ = 24 + ((PCO₂ - 40) / 10) x 1 to 2; chronic HCO₃ = 24 + ((PCO₂ - 40) / 10) x 3 to 4
abg_resp_acid_subst: (%.1f - 40) / 10 = %.2f; acute range %.2f-%.2f mmol/L; chronic range %.2f-%.2f mmol/L
abg_resp_alk_formula: Respiratory alkalosis compensation: acute HCO₃ = 24 - ((40 - PCO₂) / 10) x 1 to 2; chronic HCO₃ = 24 - ((40 - PCO₂) / 10) x 4 to 5
abg_resp_alk_subst: (40 - %.1f) / 10 = %.2f; acute range %.2f-%.2f mmol/L; chronic range %.2f-%.2f mmol/L
abg_met_alk_formula: Metabolic alkalosis compensation: expected PCO₂ = 40 + 0.7 x (HCO₃ - 24) +/- 5
abg_met_alk_subst: 40 + 0.7 x (%.1f - 24) = %.2f; expected range %.2f-%.2f mmHg; actual PCO₂ %.1f
burns_formula: 24h fluid = 4 mL x weight (kg) x TBSA (%); first 8h rate = 50% total / 8; next 16h rate = 50% total / 16
anti_dose_formula: Single dose = weight (kg) x prescribed dose (mg/kg)
insulin_tdd_formula: TDD = weight (kg) x 0.5 U/kg; basal = TDD x 50%; bolus = TDD x 50%
insulin_tdd_subst: %.2f kg x 0.5 U/kg = %.2f U; %.2f U x 50%% = %.2f U basal; %.2f U x 50%% = %.2f U bolus
infusion_rate_formula: mL/h = dose (mcg/kg/min) x weight (kg) x 60 / concentration (mcg/mL)
infusion_total_rate_formula: Concentration = total drug (mg) x 1000 / volume (mL); mL/h = dose (mcg/kg/min) x weight (kg) x 60 / concentration (mcg/mL)
infusion_total_dose_formula: Concentration = total drug (mg) x 1000 / volume (mL); Dose (mcg/kg/min) = rate (mL/h) x concentration (mcg/mL) / weight (kg) / 60
glucose_insulin_formula: Glucose (g) = Vol x Conc%; Insulin (U) = Glucose / Ratio
desc_glucose_insulin: Insulin dose to neutralize IV glucose
err_conc_invalid: Concentration must be a valid non-negative number.
err_vol_invalid: Volume must be a valid non-negative number.
err_ratio_invalid: Ratio must be a valid positive number.
